rulings.Case Title: CONS. OF JOSE CHEPITO AREAS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR First Account And Status Report Of Successor Conservator
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ANGELICA LIEBMANN LIVING TRUST DATED SEPTEMBER 10, 1991, AS AMENDED TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Construe The Terms Of The Trust And/Or To Modify The Terms Of The Trust Based On Changed Circumstances; For Confirming Trustee And Removin
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/23/2026 per counsel's request (via e-mail on 12/24/2025). If the matter is not settled before the next hearing, the following issues must be addressed: 1) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with notice of the hearing. 2) Petitioner/co-trustee resides in Kern County, while respondent/co-trustee does not reside in California. Pleae explain why this petition should not be filed in Kern County. See Probate Code section 17002(b)(2). If petitioner/co-trustee contends that the principal place of administration is in San Francisco, please identify examples of specific day-to-day activity of the trust physically carried on in San Francisco by petitioner/co-trustee (see subdivison (a)). 3) Please explain why the Court should exercise jurisdiction over respondent/co-trustee who does not reside in California, unless respondent/co-trustee makes a general appearance or otherwise consents to jurisdictiono of California court. See section 17004.
